problems.title: Pothole on our street
problems.aiSolution:
**Problem Analysis:** Road damage is a public safety hazard and authorities have legal obligation to repair it promptly.

**Root Cause:** Potholes form due to poor drainage, overloaded vehicles, aging bitumen surfaces, or substandard construction materials.

**Immediate Action:** Alert local police to place warning cones if accident-prone. File complaint on CPGRAMS with exact GPS coordinates and photos.

**Long-Term Fix:** Demand construction using CC (Cement Concrete) roads instead of bitumen for permanent solution. Push for proper drainage channels alongside road repair.

**Legal Angle:** Under Motor Vehicles Act, if an accident occurs due to a pothole, the responsible authority can be held liable for negligence. Use this as leverage in your complaint.

authority.authority: Public Works Department (PWD) / NHAI for Highways
authority.platform: MyGov Portal / CPGRAMS (cpgrams.gov.in) / State PWD website
steps: Photograph road damage with landmarks visible
Note exact GPS location and nearest landmark
File on CPGRAMS portal or PWD website
Contact local Ward Councillor for faster action
Quote Motor Vehicles Act if accident risk exists
Follow up weekly with complaint reference number

problems.title: Test Problem
problems.description: This is a test problem from restructured app
problems.aiSolution:
**Problem Analysis:** Your problem has been categorized and a structured solution plan has been created.

**Root Cause:** Civic problems typically arise from lack of accountability, resource constraints, or communication gaps between citizens and authorities.

**Immediate Action:** Document everything with timestamps, photos, and witness contacts. Rally at least 5–10 neighbors to file the same complaint — collective complaints are prioritized.

**Escalation Strategy:** Local Office → District Collector → State Grievance Portal → National CPGRAMS. Each escalation level typically gets 3× faster response.

**Power Tools:** (1) RTI — Right to Information Act to track inaction. (2) Consumer Forum for service failures. (3) Lokayukt for government negligence. Always get a complaint number and follow up in writing every 7 days.

steps: Gather evidence: photos, documents, dates, witnesses
Visit nearest government office related to your issue
File written complaint, get acknowledgment receipt
Use CPGRAMS portal for central government issues
Use State CM Helpline for state government issues
Approach Consumer Forum or Lokayukt if needed
File RTI if information is being withheld
